section_miscategorizations.text: He had a recent kidney function test and his creatinine was stable.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence reports an objective lab result (kidney function test with stable creatinine), which belongs in Findings rather than in the symptom/history narrative of the HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: He reports an A1c around 60, with fasting glucose around 130.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Although framed as patient-reported, these are specific quantitative lab values and would more appropriately be documented under Findings; however, as patient recall of prior labs, inclusion in HPI is not strongly misleading.

This repeats the A1c value already described as patient-reported in the HPI; if this is meant as a confirmed lab result it fits Findings, but the duplication and lack of clarification make its placement somewhat confusing rather than clearly wrong.
